top_stories.title: Sparse charging stations slowing Filipinos’ shift to EV
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Although the war-driven rise in fuel prices has stoked greater interest in electric vehicles (EVs) in the Philippines, the country’s still-limited charging infrastructure risks slowing that transition, according to a study by global advisory firm Deloitte. top_stories.title: Pinoys show growing interest in hybrids, EVs – study
top_stories.summary: The Philippines is seeing growing consumer interest in hybrid and electric vehicles as motorists seek to cut fuel costs, but limited infrastructure continues to hinder wider adoption, according to a study by professional services firm Deloitte.

secondary_signals.title: Diesel, gas poised for new price jumps on May 26
secondary_signals.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Motorists may have to cough up more money when refueling as new price hikes are seen next week on May 26. Estimates from an industry source showed a P1 to P1.30 per liter increase for diesel. Gasoline may also have an upward adjustment of P1.20 to P1.50 per liter. READ: House OKs bill
